68, trapped bears, and ripped over 150 Points into Monday’s 6913 highs. Yesterday, we did it again. I wrote Tuesday at 4pm:

highlights.text: I wrote Tuesday at 4pm: “Bear case tomorrow: 6832 has to fail, but in advance of that there is is a complex short on the failure of 6873.” We lost 6832 early Tuesday morning, broke & went elevator down Tuesday to the 6718 daily low.
My general lean is ES can try up the range more.” Today we made it to 6890s after yet another trap below the range overnight.
Readers know I trade mostly one setup: The Failed Breakdown, and we’ve seen it lots.
Failed Breakdowns start usually when price goes elevator down in a vertical flush cutting through every support.

As always, the gap down will result in a Failed Breakdown/squeeze but I would not hold any size over the weekend.” This was highly prescient, and on early Saturday morning, this risk materialized and we gapped down Sunday 45 points.
Like all gap downs/elevator down sells in ES though, this did not take long to see a Failed Breakdown and rip.
Like every elevator down sell though, it would resolve with a short squeeze.
I wrote yesterday at 4pm: “At 11am ES put in a big Failed Breakdown of the 6743 low we set at 6:15AM.

Dips are getting bought again for SPX, but can Bulls follow through?.In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow.

post_title: Dips Are Getting Bought Again For SPX, But Can Bulls Follow Through? March 5 Plan
